Title: Missing enzymes in the biosynthesis of the anticancer drug vinblastine in Madagascar periwinkle

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Madagascar periwinkle; Nicotiana benthamiana; Pichia pastoris; Sf9 insect cells
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Catharanthus roseus; Nicotiana benthamiana; Pichia pastoris; Spodoptera frugiperda
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Catharanthine and tabersonine are monomeric precursors for bioactive monoterpene indole alkaloids; vinblastine is a potent anticancer drug
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Branch from stemmadenine acetate to catharanthine/tabersonine scaffolds
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Reticuline oxidase homolog; berberine bridge enzyme-like flavin-dependent oxidase
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Vacuole via ER-derived vesicles; likely ER lumen/ER-derived vacuole-targeted vesicles/vacuole
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low absolute expression but similar tissue expression pattern to TS and other vinblastine biosynthetic enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes stemmadenine acetate to precondylocarpine acetate via C-N bond oxidation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of catharanthine and tabersonine, thereby enabling vinblastine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream redox step before DPAS and CS/TS cyclization branch
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key missing pathway step completing the scaffold-forming sequence; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes conversion of stemmadenine acetate into precondylocarpine acetate and is required for downstream catharanthine/tabersonine formation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Silencing of the oxidase in C. roseus caused accumulation of stemmadenine acetate, implicating the gene in its consumption. 2. Heterologous PAS produced precondylocarpine acetate from stemmadenine acetate in vitro. 3. Coexpression of PAS with DPAS and CS or TS in N. benthamiana enabled catharanthine or tabersonine production, respectively.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS in C. roseus; transient coexpression in N. benthamiana; in vitro enzyme assay with heterologous protein; LC-MS/coelution with semisynthetic standard; proteomics validation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq from C. roseus (accession NA); proteomics data S2 and S3
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PAS did not react with stemmadenine lacking the acetyl group, indicating acetyl-group recognition. PAS lacks the His and Cys residues involved in covalent FAD binding in canonical berberine bridge enzymes.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for heterologous reconstruction of catharanthine/tabersonine/vinblastine pathways and engineering production platforms for valuable alkaloids

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Madagascar periwinkle; Nicotiana benthamiana; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Catharanthus roseus; Nicotiana benthamiana; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Catharanthine and tabersonine are precursors of diverse bioactive alkaloids; vinblastine is an anticancer drug
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Reduction step from precondylocarpine acetate toward dehydrosecodine precursor
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces precondylocarpine acetate to the labile intermediate proposed as dihydroprecondylocarpine acetate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor that is converted by CS or TS to catharanthine or tabersonine
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream redox step between PAS and CS/TS
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key missing pathway step; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required to convert precondylocarpine acetate into the unstable intermediate feeding catharanthine/tabersonine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Silencing of the alcohol dehydrogenase in C. roseus caused accumulation of precondylocarpine acetate. 2. DPAS together with PAS and CS/TS reconstructed catharanthine or tabersonine formation in N. benthamiana and in vitro. 3. DPAS preferentially interacted with TS, suggesting possible flux control toward tabersonine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene silencing in C. roseus; transient coexpression in N. benthamiana; in vitro coupled enzyme assays; LC-MS/MS comparison with synthetic standards; BiFC
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The product could not be isolated because of lability and was inferred from degradation product tubotaiwine and coupled assay outcomes.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential component for pathway reconstitution in synthetic biology platforms producing monoterpene indole alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reactive intermediate may decompose or cyclize spontaneously, complicating engineering

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Madagascar periwinkle; Nicotiana benthamiana; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Catharanthus roseus; Nicotiana benthamiana; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Tabersonine is a precursor of vindoline and other bioactive alkaloids; vinblastine is anticancer
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Monoterpene indole alkaloid / tabersonine branch of vinblastine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Shared expression profile with previously identified vinblastine biosynthetic enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Dihydroprecondylocarpine acetate (proposed); precondylocarpine acetate in coupled assay context
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Desacetoxylates/catalyzes conversion of the unstable dihydroprecondylocarpine acetate intermediate to dehydrosecodine that cyclizes to tabersonine
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the tabersonine scaffold, an immediate branch precursor toward vindoline/vinblastine
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal branch-determining step for tabersonine scaffold formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes tabersonine formation from the shared unstable intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VIGS of TS in C. roseus significantly reduced tabersonine accumulation. 2. TS alone did not react with stemmadenine or stemmadenine acetate, clarifying that its substrate is a downstream unstable intermediate. 3. Together with PAS and DPAS, TS reconstituted tabersonine biosynthesis in N. benthamiana and in vitro.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS; heterologous expression; coupled in vitro enzyme assays; transient coexpression in N. benthamiana; LC-MS/coelution with standard; BiFC
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TS and CS likely retained hydrolase activity from an ancestral hydrolase and use the same unstable substrate. TS showed stronger interaction with DPAS than CS did.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for directing flux specifically toward tabersonine-derived alkaloids in heterologous hosts
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Spontaneous cyclization of intermediates may reduce branch specificity

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Madagascar periwinkle; Nicotiana benthamiana; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Catharanthus roseus; Nicotiana benthamiana; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Catharanthine is a precursor of vinblastine/vincristine and other bioactive alkaloids; vinblastine is anticancer
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Monoterpene indole alkaloid / catharanthine branch of vinblastine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Shared expression profile with previously identified vinblastine biosynthetic enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Dihydroprecondylocarpine acetate (proposed); precondylocarpine acetate in coupled assay context
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Desacetoxylates/catalyzes conversion of the unstable dihydroprecondylocarpine acetate intermediate to dehydrosecodine that cyclizes to catharanthine
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms catharanthine, a direct monomeric precursor of vinblastine
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal branch-determining step for catharanthine scaffold formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes catharanthine formation from the shared unstable intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VIGS of CS in C. roseus significantly reduced catharanthine accumulation. 2. CS alone did not react with stemmadenine or stemmadenine acetate, indicating a downstream unstable substrate. 3. Together with PAS and DPAS, CS reconstituted catharanthine biosynthesis in N. benthamiana and in vitro.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS; heterologous expression; coupled in vitro enzyme assays; transient coexpression in N. benthamiana; LC-MS/coelution with standard; BiFC
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CS and TS likely act on the same unstable precursor but direct alternative cyclization outcomes. CS interacted more weakly with DPAS than TS did.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for directing flux toward catharanthine and downstream vinblastine/vincristine production in engineered hosts
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Branch competition with tabersonine formation and spontaneous cyclization may complicate control
